feedback.text: Hello, and thank you for your time and attention. I would add to the four stated key points of the DFA the express aim of protecting consumers from having their paid-for purchases from being disabled at the sole discretion of the service provider. In the case of video games in particular, the legislation lags behind in this regard. As things stand, a customer might find themselves paying for a product which they are then deprived of, with no recourse for compensation, nor any advance notice at the time of purchase. The state of consumer rights has a blind spot when it comes to video games, and I would appreciate it if you would investigate this matter and work toward addressing the issue. Best regards, ~TL
